title: Savory Flaky Chinese Pork Wonton Soup
description: A citrusy and comforting Chinese dish featuring wild garlic leaves, perfect for a sick day, made with high protein wontons wrapped in corn husks and simmered in a savory broth.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine ground pork shoulder, chopped wild garlic leaves, soy sauce, and sesame oil, and mix well until just combined.
Lay a corn husk flat and place a tablespoon of the pork mixture in the center of the husk.
Fold the bottom half of the corn husk over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll up the husk to form a tight cylinder, and repeat with the remaining husks and filling.
In a large pot, combine chicken broth, sliced ginger, and chopped green onions, and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to low and add the wontons to the pot, making sure they are not crowded.
Simmer the wontons for 10 minutes, or until they float to the surface.
Serve the wontons in bowls with the hot broth spooned over the top.
Garnish with additional chopped green onions and serve immediately.
